title: Rei likes her big guns
wikitext:
[]: Rei likes her big guns Post by Iron Rooster » Wed Jun 04, 2008 11:16 pm I'll leave it at that. . .

[]: Post by Sachi » Wed Jun 04, 2008 11:24 pm Rei is usually the supporting unit. So she would have the big, long ranged weapon.

[]: Post by Iron Rooster » Wed Jun 04, 2008 11:36 pm Good point, though in that pic with Armisael Rei is definitely the main force. Then again, at this point in the storyline, things are starting to go nuts. I could see Rei being a marksman.

[]: Post by Indigo Arcangel » Thu Jun 05, 2008 4:18 am I could see Rei being a marksman.

[]: Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Thu Jun 05, 2008 6:47 am In my opinion big guns really suit her for some reason in the anime series. I can clearly picture Eva-00 holding up a Barret 50cal M82, kicking some tight ass. And many of the Angels Eva-00 versed were "big", in a sort of way, and I think that required some heavy weaponry.

[]: Post by Xard » Thu Jun 05, 2008 7:07 am tbh Rei has mindset of sniper

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jun 05, 2008 9:18 am Indigo Arcangel wrote: Another idea for tenant of room 404 - Rei with Barret m82 = PURE AWSUM

[]: Post by Squirrel Ninja » Thu Jun 05, 2008 10:52 am It's a little nostalgic of

[]: Post by BobBQ » Thu Jun 05, 2008 10:53 am UrsusArctos wrote: Rei could do with an Arctic Warfare Magnum- if I remember right, that's the gun the Eva weapon was based on.

[]: Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu Jun 05, 2008 10:55 am Yay guns. Yea, I think it's because 00 is a support/experimental type and wasn't built for any real combat.

[]: Post by mrpanda » Thu Jun 05, 2008 12:24 pm If your using an awm, go for the british army version the L115A3. The British Army adopted the AWSM rifle chambered in .338 Lapua Magnum as the L115A1 Long Range Rifle. The British L115A1 rifles are outfitted with Schmidt & Bender 3-12x50 PM II telescopic sights. The L115A1 is currently (2007) in service with the British Army and Royal Marines in Afghanistan and Iraq. In November 2007 the British Ministry of Defence (MOD) announced that their snipers in the Army, Royal Marines and RAF Regiment are to get a new rifle. Accuracy International will supply 580 L115A3 Long Range Rifles with day telescopic sights. The L115A3 is being supplied as part of a broader Sniper System Improvement (SSI) program which also includes night sights, spotting scopes, laser range finders and tripods. The L115A3 rifle is due to be ready for operational use in the spring of 2008.[10][11] The upgrade over the L115A1 comprises mounting: * Schmidt & Bender 5-25x56 PM II LP telescopic sights; * Suppressors to reduce the flash and noise signature; * Folding stocks for better carrying in a backpack; * Adjustable cheek pieces for more comfort and better eye alignment with the telescopic sight; * Butt spikes to aid stability during firing; * Adjustable bipods, which differ from the original Accuracy International bipod; * 5 round box magazines. The MOD claims a muzzle velocity of 936 m/s (3071 ft/s) for the L115A3.[12] This implies the British military are using a high pressure load to attain this muzzle velocity. A reverse engineering simulation with QuickLOAD internal ballistic software predicted that a typical military .338 Lapua Magnum load using Lapua LockBase B408 bullets at an overall cartridge length of 91.44 mm (3.600 in) produces ≈ 460 MPa (66833 psi) maximum piezo chamber pressure to achieve the claimed 936 m/s (3071 ft/s) muzzle velocity.

[]: Post by Dartz » Thu Jun 05, 2008 1:01 pm I was hoping for a very different set of guns...

[]: Post by slothen » Thu Jun 05, 2008 1:38 pm I like Rei's big guns too.

[]: Post by Indigo Arcangel » Thu Jun 05, 2008 3:21 pm UrsusArctos wrote: OT- There are bigger, nastier guns than the M82. Rei could do with an Arctic Warfare Magnum- if I remember right, that's the gun the Eva weapon was based on.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jun 05, 2008 7:32 pm Have you heard of the Gepard, Indigo? It's a hungarian AM rifle that comes in three calibers- one of which is the mighty 14.5mm Russian, with twice as much energy as a .50 cal! And it's supposed to be even more accurate than the M82. And the Finns developed a super Anti-Tank rifle in World War Two, the Lahti L-39. Not a sniper weapon, but it hits like hell. (Dear me, I'd better not post any more replies in this thread- or move this to OT)

[]: Post by RandomStuff » Thu Jun 05, 2008 9:15 pm Xard wrote: tbh Rei has mindset of sniper

[]: Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 05, 2008 10:42 pm ITT gun nerdery

[]: Post by Iron Rooster » Thu Jun 05, 2008 10:55 pm Isn't it funny how little Eva guns actually kill anything? I think the only angels that were killed by guns were Ramiel, Gaghiel (Ship guns), and Matariel. That first pic really demonstrates just how powerful an AT Field is. It stopped a point blank sniper rifle shot from Rei. Not gun related: Then Shinji's bowie knife goes right in like Armisael was made of butter. Hmmmm . . . that is one nice knife. Maybe someone should make a knife for general use that's strong enough to cut through AT Fields. Only angel that had an AT Field strong enough to stop a prog knive is Kaworu. Damn him!

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jun 05, 2008 11:49 pm Reichu wrote: In dieses thread, Besessenegewehrenliebe.

[]: Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Fri Jun 06, 2008 12:33 am Squirrel Ninja wrote: It's a little nostalgic of [SERAAAASSS! YAAAYYYY! bangbangbangbangbangbangbang]
